TimeEntry.Title: wkorx02,WKORX72,wkorx70,wkorx67,wkorx65,wkorx64,wkorx63,wkorx61,WKORX54,wkorx53,wkorx52,wkorx50,wkorx48: started sync with OPS manager
TimeEntry.Notes:
I accessed the OPS Manager portal and found that the following machines were not synced for a long time: WKORX02, WKORX72, WKORX70, WKORX67, WKORX65, WKORX64, WKORX63, WKORX61, WKORX54, WKORX53, WKORX52, WKORX50, and WKORX48.

During diagnosis, I found that the SNMP protocol was not installed on some of the machines, possibly due to recent formatting. I installed the latest version of SNMP, configured the required SNMP thread settings, removed the old records of these machines from the portal, and then rediscovered and added them again.

After checking again an hour later, all machines were properly synced with OPS Manager and were showing alerts correctly.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Network setup after reboot firewall and switch
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary / Resolution Notes

Worked with Sai and Brett on the issue. Brett changed the power source of the switch and firewall to the UPS, after which all systems did not come back online.

Requested Brett to connect his system using a mobile hotspot. Once connected, I accessed his system and ran a network scan to identify the ESXi host where the firewall was located.

Verified that Sophos firewall was online. Checked, re-seated, and verified all network cables. Reconfigured network settings as needed and confirmed link status was up.

Verified that all systems were receiving internet connectivity and were operational.

Issue resolved and ticket closed.

TimeEntry.Title: Offboarding - Flores, Jazmin
TimeEntry.Notes: Offboarding for the client was completed, including removing the user profile and data from the workstation, cleaning up disk space, and uninstalling unnecessary apps. The user account was disabled and archived in Active Directory, and the user was removed from all 3CX call queues and had their extension deleted. Outlook was disabled, and emails were archived. The user was removed from all distribution lists, including Collections and Customer Service, and the email address was checked to ensure they weren’t in any other groups. MS 365 license was disabled. MS Teams access was revoked, and the user was removed from all teams and groups. The final step left is to disable and delete the OXPLive account, which Brett manages personally.

TimeEntry.Title: 3CX Unregistered Users
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per request received from Brett 3cx Users Yolanda Sanchez (5541),Jasmine Quintana (5551),Robin Brown (5556),Shantle Jones (5673),Denise Linares (5652)  extensions were showing as unregistered on 3CX admin console. I have reviewed the unregistered users listed in message, and here's the update:

The machines were in an idle state, and 3CX was showing the users as unregistered. However, when I logged into the user machines with their credentials, 3CX automatically registered the users on both the machine and the 3CX portal. 

 

 

  • The following users are now successfully registered on their respective machines after I logged in with their user credentials:

    • Jasmine Quintana (5551) – Registered on JRMED08.
    • Robin Brown (5556) – Registered on WKORX66.
    • Shantle Jones (5673) – Registered on WKORX-02.

TimeEntry.Title: Delete Email
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Title: Request to Delete or Recall Email and Attachment

Description:
Brett Varner inquired about the possibility of deleting an email sent on Monday, January 5th at 12:02 PM by Dawn Bowe with the subject "Annual Evaluations - Due FEBRUARY 4th" from all recipients' mailboxes, or at least removing the attachment.

  • I tried searching and using the Compliance Center to recall the email, but it was not successful as the email was sent a while ago.

  • Unfortunately, it is not possible to delete the email or the attachment once it has already been sent and received by the recipients.

  • The attachment cannot be deleted from recipients' mailboxes, as there's no direct way to remove it once the email is delivered.

Status: Closed
Action Taken: Updated Brett with the details and closed the ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Brett Reqested that user having issue while upload link is not accessible for user pjimenez
TimeEntry.Notes: The user reported an issue with file uploads in SharePoint. While external users were able to upload files using the provided link, internal user pjimenez encountered a permission denied error when attempting to upload. After investigating permissions and other settings in SharePoint, I opened a case with Microsoft Support to explain the scenario. The support agent confirmed that everything appeared to be functioning correctly from the portal. I then attempted to connect to the user's machine; however, the user was busy and requested to reconnect later. TimeEntry.Title: DESKTOP Alert - Critical - JRMED10 Disk Utilization
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the alert connected to JRMED10, download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. 39% of the space is free. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.

TimeEntry.Title: WKORX 70: Konica Minolta - Printer Preferences
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: Konica Minolta bizhub C308 Printer Driver Setup and Troubleshooting

Description

Tasked with installing and configuring the printer driver for the Konica Minolta bizhub C308.

Actions Taken

  • Downloaded the latest available Konica Minolta printer drivers for Windows from the official site.

  • During installation, noticed that the exact C308 model was not listed in the wizard. Selected KONICA MINOLTA C368SeriesPS driver, as it is officially compatible with the C308.

  • Encountered multiple errors during the installation process.

  • Troubleshooting Steps:

    • Cleaned up all previously installed or failed printer driver packages from the system.

    • Rebooted the workstation to ensure all changes were applied and no remnants remained.

    • Ran Windows Update and verified the system was current.

    • Attempted the driver installation again; this time, the process completed successfully.

  • Finalized printer setup and performed test prints to confirm functionality.

Status

  • Printer driver installed, printer configured, and all features tested.

  • Ticket closed.

TimeEntry.Title: ORX - Azure AD Work
TimeEntry.Notes:
We were tasked with creating a dynamic user security group in Microsoft Entra ID to automatically include enabled internal users with an Exchange Online mailbox (Plan 1 or Plan 2). Initial attempts to create the group via the Entra portal were unsuccessful when the dynamic rule included license-based conditions using user.assignedPlans, even though the rule syntax validated correctly. Permissions and Entra ID P1 licensing were reviewed and confirmed to be in place.

To isolate the issue, a simplified dynamic rule without license checks was tested and created successfully, confirming that dynamic groups were functioning. The issue was specific to license-based conditions in the portal. As a workaround, the group was created using Microsoft Graph PowerShell, which bypassed the portal limitation and completed successfully.

After creation, the dynamic membership rule was verified in the portal, and rule processing was confirmed to be active. Membership evaluation was tested by removing and reassigning an Exchange license to a user, and the group membership updated automatically as expected. The dynamic group is now working as intended.

TimeEntry.Title: Unable to Connect to RDP
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:
Verified the CloudBrink connector settings and tested the connection from a local host. The host was reachable via IP address, but not by hostname, indicating a DNS-related issue. Reviewed and verified all connector settings in the CloudBrink portal. Engaged CloudBrink support and, during the support session, reviewed logs and configurations in detail. Reconfigured the connector with correct DNS settings and tested successfully; connectivity is now working as expected.

Ticket will remain open pending confirmation and verification from Brett.

TimeEntry.Title: LPJRM01 - Dawn Bowe
TimeEntry.Notes: There was an ongoing issue for Dawn Bowe, which was initially being handled by Sanjeev. While Sanjeev was on break, I picked up Dawn’s call. She reported issues with her network connectivity and Adobe. Upon checking Adobe Acrobat, I found that her license was not being recognized and the application was showing as a free trial, preventing her from editing documents. I signed her out of Adobe, confirmed the correct account, and signed her back in; however, the application continued to show a free trial. Dawn mentioned that she has an Adobe license key on a CD, so I asked her to connect the CD adapter through her docking station. We then discovered that the CD adapter was not being detected by the computer, which likely explains why the LAN/Ethernet port was also not functioning. I collected the docking station’s model and version details and noted that the docking station was not showing its usual indicator light. I shared all gathered information with Sanjeev and Ajay for further investigation and follow-up.

TimeEntry.Title: Dawn bowe Call regarding login issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Received a call from Dawn Bowie regarding login issues on her domain-joined computer. Logged in remotely and instructed her to use her correct Microsoft account password, allowing successful sign-in. After login, shortcuts and files were missing, so OneDrive was verified and synced, restoring her files and desktop shortcuts.

TimeEntry.Title: LPJRM01 - Dawn Bowe
TimeEntry.Notes: s per the handover from Sanjeev, Adobe needed to be set up. As per ajay, the Adobe Drive upgrade from the old version was required. The installer initially got stuck. The device was rebooted and the installer was re-run, but it continued to fail. Event Viewer was reviewed, and a missing .NET dependency was identified. The required .NET package was installed, the installer was re-run, and Adobe Drive installed successfully. Verified functionality and confirmed the issue is resolved.

TimeEntry.Title: New Hire: Flores, Nancy
TimeEntry.Notes: I completed the new-hire user onboarding process and workstation configuration tasks. This included creating the user’s Active Directory account, verifying synchronization to Microsoft 365, assigning the required M365 Standard license, and adding the user to all required Microsoft Teams groups. The assigned workstation was prepared by signing in with the new account, removing outdated local profiles where possible, and applying all standard configuration settings. Microsoft 365 and Office desktop apps were installed and configured, and Teams was set up with proper group access. Google Chrome was configured as the default browser and all required organizational bookmarks were added. The 3CX softphone was provisioned for the user and added to the appropriate call queues. Outlook was configured with the necessary shared inboxes, and the Konica printer for the Palmdale location was added and tested for both print and scan-to-email functionality. Standard security checks were completed, including ensuring OEM antivirus was disabled. Windows taskbar cleanup was performed to remove unnecessary items and pin required applications. Removal of the previous user’s local account on the workstation remains pending because their domain account is still active; this will be completed once deactivation is confirmed. All other configurations were completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: New Hire: Valerie Estrada
TimeEntry.Notes: I completed the new-hire user onboarding process and workstation configuration tasks. This included creating the user’s Active Directory account, verifying synchronization to Microsoft 365, assigning the required M365 Standard license, and adding the user to all required Microsoft Teams groups. The assigned workstation was prepared by signing in with the new account, removing outdated local profiles where possible, and applying all standard configuration settings. Microsoft 365 and Office desktop apps were installed and configured, and Teams was set up with proper group access. Google Chrome was configured as the default browser and all required organizational bookmarks were added. The 3CX softphone was provisioned for the user and added to the appropriate call queues. Outlook was configured with the necessary shared inboxes, and the Konica printer for the Palmdale location was added and tested for both print and scan-to-email functionality. Standard security checks were completed, including ensuring OEM antivirus was disabled. Windows taskbar cleanup was performed to remove unnecessary items and pin required applications. All other configurations were completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: WKORX72 - Virus dectected
TimeEntry.Notes:
User, Lisa, reported a virus alert triggered by McAfee virus protection. The user was unable to access a website and received a security warning...

I connected to Lisa's system remotely and performed a full security scan using our Technijian security tools, which found no threats. Also verified tha McAfee is not installed on the computer.

Went to the browser settings and enalbed the pop-up blocker. After completing these steps, I followed up with Lisa, and she confirmed that she was now able to access the same website without any issues or further alerts.

It appears that the issue was caused by a false positive from McAfee's default virus protection. The problem was resolved, and the user is now able to access the website as expected.

TimeEntry.Title: Daisybill
TimeEntry.Notes:
- ran the program, Had issues initially with the page not found, fixed that.
- after successfull sso login. because of role permisiions, it will not let me to login. So I had to edit the role in azure app registration.
- I have been usign the same app registration for couple of other projects, I didnt want to mess that up. So Created a new azure app registration and configured with daisybill mcp
-I resolved multiple frontend and auth issues: fixed an HTTPS mismatch by switching the Vite dev server to HTTP and correcting CORS origins; resolved a React routing error by reordering providers so BrowserRouter wraps AuthProvider; and fixed Azure AD login by adding the SPA redirect URI (http://localhost:3000), configuring app roles and API scopes, and assigning the user in Enterprise Applications. I also fixed UI API 404s by routing requests through /api using VITE_API_BASE_URL. Current blocker is that all /api/* calls return 500 errors—next step is checking MCPAdminPortal logs/health and verifying the SQL Server (10.100.106.72), database (OrxDaisyBillMcp), and required DB scripts (001–005) are fully applied.
TimeEntry.Resource: T. Shoban Babu IRV-PR1 Develop - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: New Distribution Email: HSC@orthoxpress.com
TimeEntry.Notes:
Got the request from Brett that Need to restrict the user that can send to distribution group....

so tried to add the user howerver the distribution group is currently created in a hybrid Exchange environment, directly in Active Directory (AD). Since it was created in AD, it does not support the functionality to restrict senders.so found that

On-Premises Exchange Server Requirement:
In our hybrid setup, distribution groups that are synchronized from on-premises Active Directory to Exchange Online cannot have sender restrictions managed via Exchange Online PowerShell. The Message Delivery Restrictions feature, which is needed to limit who can send emails to the distribution group, must be configure

d in the Exchange Management Shell (EMS), which is only available on on-premises Exchange servers.

Absence of EMS:
Since we do not have an on-premises Exchange Server or Exchange Management Shell (EMS) in our environment, we are unable to directly configure Message Delivery Restrictions for distribution groups that are synchronized from on-premises Active Directory. This functionality requires access to an Exchange server, which we currently do not have.

Exchange Online Limitations:
The Exchange Online PowerShell module is designed for managing cloud-based Exchange resources and does not support configurations like Message Delivery Restrictions for distribution groups that are synchronized from on-premises Exchange. This functionality is exclusive to on-prem Exchange environments.

we performed a test in Exchange Online, confirming that only the specified users were able to send messages to the distribution group, while all others were blocked. This test was successful, and the changes have been implemented as requested. Waiting for the brett response....

TimeEntry.Title: RE: No access to QB
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per Brett’s email, I sent a remote session request to Rick. Rick responded and requested to temporarily disable Norton 360, for which I provided the required instructions. After that, I successfully connected to the system via remote session.

Once connected, I logged in to the user’s machine to understand the issue and observed that the previous session was not properly terminated. I then logged in to the server, checked the QuickBooks login status, logged off the company profile, and terminated the active session.

Afterward, I asked the user to reconnect to the remote session and log in to QuickBooks again. The user was able to log in successfully, and the issue worked as expected.

Hence, the issue has been resolved and the ticket is now closed.

TimeEntry.Title: 3CX issues
TimeEntry.Notes:
**Ticket Summary: Outgoing Call Issue with 3CX Client**

* Nancy reported issues with outgoing calls on the 3CX client.
* Upon investigation, I found that the problem was likely due to a corrupted or outdated client configuration.
* I checked the 3CX portal to confirm the client's registration status and reviewed the PBX logs for any discrepancies.
* I then verified the client’s network and audio device settings to ensure there were no misconfigurations.
* After confirming the settings, I proceeded with reinstalling the 3CX client, which cleared the cached settings and forced the client to re-register with the PBX.
* After reinstalling, the outgoing call functionality was restored successfully.

Issue has been resolved, and outgoing calls are now functioning as expected.

---

TimeEntry.Title: TRYING TO OBTAIN VOICEMAIL FROM PHONE
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged into user machine to access the phones for client. Because the voice mails are not working on Desk phones. After that start troubleshooting the issue and do the following setups to fix the issues.

The voicemail setup and access issue affecting Dawn Bowe and Juan Hernandez’s 3CX desk phones has been successfully resolved. Here’s a summary of the steps I took to address and fix the issue:

  1. Reprovisioned Users: I reprovisioned both users in the 3CX admin portal to ensure their configurations were correct.
  2. Phone Configuration: The phones were reconfigured multiple times to ensure proper setup, especially for voicemail and outbound dialing.
  3. Factory Reset: I performed a factory reset on both phones to clear any potential configuration conflicts.
  4. Auto Provisioning: The phones were auto-provisioned to apply the latest settings.
  5. Firmware Upgrade: I upgraded the firmware on the phones to the latest version for better performance and compatibility.

The issue has been tested and confirmed as resolved with both Dawn and Juan. Everything is functioning as expected now.

TimeEntry.Title: Remote Desktop
TimeEntry.Notes:
Problem: Lisa experienced an issue where Outlook wouldn’t open while using Remote Desktop (RDP).

Steps Taken:

Logged into Lisa’s office machine and then accessed Remote Desktop.

Outlook was not opening, so I ended tasks on the distribution server, signed Lisa out, and logged her back in.

The issue persisted, so I created a new profile in Outlook from control panel, but it still didn’t work.

As Lisa needed to continue working, I provided instructions on using Outlook on the web and how to add a shared mailbox.

Lisa was able to proceed with work through Outlook Web temporarily as I gave her the instructions to use it in web.

Next Steps:

We planned to address the RDP issue during off-hours for a more in-depth troubleshooting session.

TimeEntry.Title: Manual Patch Installation and Resolution for Machines LPORX02, WKORX54, LPJRM01, WKORX45
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Title:
Manual Patch Installation and Resolution for Machines LPORX02, WKORX54, LPJRM01, WKORX45

Issue Summary:

  • Failed patches were detected on machines LPORX02, WKORX54, LPJRM01, and WKORX45. These machines were unable to install the necessary patches automatically, resulting in missing or outdated patches that needed manual intervention.

Actions Taken:

  1. Logged into ManageEngine Portal:

    • Checked the failed or missing patches for the specified machines (LPORX02, WKORX54, LPJRM01, WKORX45).

  2. Manual Patch Installation:

    • Downloaded and installed the applicable patches manually that were failing to install automatically on the machines.

  3. Scan for Installed Patches:

    • After installing the patches, performed a scan to check for the installed patches on the machines.

  4. Verification on MY RMM Portal:

    • Verified the machines on the MY RMM portal and found a new pending patch for installation.

    • Downloaded and installed the pending patch.

  5. Final Scan:

    • After the installation of the pending patch, conducted a final scan to ensure all patches were successfully installed.

  6. Successful Update:

    • All failed patches were successfully updated, and the machines are now in a healthy state.

TimeEntry.Title: JRMEDSVR-01 Setup and Configuration:
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

The current setup involves working with the JRMEDSVR-01 server, which needs to be integrated into the network by addressing several key components:

  1. Scan Setup for JRMED Old Server:

    • The scan functionality needs to be configured to ensure proper communication and scanning between the new setup and the old server.

    • The JRMEDSVR-01 server is pointing to the old DNS and DHCP setup, which needs to be fixed for smooth network operations.

  2. DHCP Configuration:

    • The old server currently holds the DHCP and DNS roles.

    • The firewall has been configured with the DHCP pool, but DNS and DHCP settings need to be fixed to ensure proper IP address allocation and name resolution across the network.

    • The DNS and DHCP settings on the firewall must be aligned with the correct configurations for the JRMED setup.

  3. DNS Configuration Fix:

    • The DNS configuration for the old server needs to be addressed, as it is currently pointing to the old DNS, which could lead to network resolution issues.

    • DNS records need to be fixed and aligned across the network to ensure proper resolution.

  4. Access Point Configuration:

    • The Access Points currently point to the old DNS server.

    • The access point configuration needs to be reviewed and updated to point to the correct DNS server, as it is still referencing the outdated configuration.

  5. Cloudbrink Initial Setup:

    • The Cloudbrink configuration has been completed but cannot be fully finalized until the DNS and DHCP issues are resolved.

    • DNS and DHCP must be fixed first to ensure that Cloudbrink functions correctly within the environment.

TimeEntry.Title: WKORX70 - help clear error printing in konica minolta
TimeEntry.Notes: I received an email from Gretchen regarding a printing issue. I connected to her system and checked the issue, where I found that the print command had been stuck. I attempted to delete the current print command but was unable to. After rebooting the system, I successfully deleted the print command. However, when I sent a test page command, it got stuck again. Upon further troubleshooting, I found that the printer was connected via an IPP port. I updated the printer's configuration to use a TCP/IP port (192.168.0.57) instead of the IPP port. After checking again, the printer started working properly. I then asked Gretchen to verify, and she sent some test prints which confirmed the printer was working. Afterward, Gretchen requested assistance with the new Outlook version. I investigated and found that MailStore was not working with the new Outlook. I provided her with the MailStore archive link from the server, and she was able to access all archived emails successfully. With the issues resolved, I am closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: New Hire: Barbosa, Marcelo
TimeEntry.Notes:
I completed the account setup for Marcelo Barbosa, starting with the Active Directory (AD) account creation under username mbarbosa. After the AD account was successfully created, I added the user to the Junior Sales Associates distribution list from AD.

Next, I assigned a Basic MS365 License to the user, followed by adding them to the All Company, Employee Resources, Operations Manual, and Sales Resources MS Teams groups. I created the 3CX extension 5675 and assigned a new DID with SMS capability. Also there were some issue regarding sales resources so I check on AD and 365 but could not find so we asked brett and moved on with adding the user to sales teams.

The user’s email, mbarbosa@orthoxpress.com
, is set up, and I will ensure the 3CX Welcome email is sent at 7 am on the start date, 2/2/2026.
